_comment: Sapphire scientific tool catalog — used by the orchestrator's Claude-driven tool-selection step in the PLAN stage. Each entry describes one selectable scientific tool. ALWAYS-ON core agents (moat, EMET, internal-science-lead, all semantic fact agents, scientific-reasoning, rescue-mechanism) are NOT listed here — they run unconditionally. Only the 8 scientific specialty tools below go through the Claude selection step.

tools.name: ASO Acute-Tox Screener
tools.purpose: Predict acute toxicity risk for antisense oligonucleotide (ASO) candidate sequences using a validated GBR model.
required_inputs: ASO nucleotide sequences (pure uppercase A/T/G/C, ≥15 nt)
tools.when_to_use: SELECT when the query involves ASO therapy, oligonucleotide design, or when explicit ASO sequences are present in the query or context. SKIP for standard gene-target or small-molecule queries with no ASO sequences.

tools.name: Boltz-2 Structure + Binding Affinity
tools.purpose: Predict protein 3D structure and ligand binding affinity (pKd/pIC50) using Boltz-2 via the hosted Boltz Compute API.
required_inputs: Target protein sequence (≥25 uppercase amino-acid letters) and/or ligand SMILES or CCD code
tools.when_to_use: SELECT when the query asks about protein structure, binding affinity, molecular docking, or when a protein sequence or SMILES ligand is present. SKIP for standard gene-level epidemiology or regulatory queries where no structural/binding input is available.

tools.name: Q-Models Runner (ESM-2 / DTI / Variant)
tools.purpose: Run Quiver's quantitative prediction models: ESM-2 protein family classification, drug-target interaction (DTI) prediction, and variant effect scoring.
required_inputs: For ESM-2/variant: gene symbol or protein sequence. For DTI: SMILES string and target gene symbol.
tools.when_to_use: SELECT when the query involves protein function prediction, drug-target binding prediction, or variant effect scoring. Most useful for gene-target queries (ESM-2 family) or small-molecule/repurposing queries (DTI). SKIP for non-gene/non-SMILES regulatory or epidemiology queries.

tools.name: gnomAD Gene Constraint
tools.purpose: Fetch quantitative gene constraint metrics from gnomAD: pLI (loss-of-function intolerance), LOEUF, and missense Z-score.
tools.when_to_use: SELECT when the query involves a named gene target and the question bears on genetic constraint, haploinsufficiency, or population-level variation tolerance. SKIP for SMILES-only, ASO-only, or non-gene queries.

tools.name: GTEx Tissue Expression
tools.purpose: Retrieve median TPM expression values and CNS-selectivity scores for a gene across GTEx tissues.
tools.when_to_use: SELECT when the query involves a named gene target and tissue-specific expression, CNS expression patterns, or target selectivity are relevant. SKIP for SMILES-only, ASO-only, or non-gene queries.

tools.name: InterPro Protein Domain Annotations
tools.purpose: Retrieve InterPro domain, family, and active-site annotations (IPR accessions) for a gene/protein.
tools.when_to_use: SELECT when the query involves a named gene target and protein domain architecture, druggable pocket identification, or functional family classification are relevant. SKIP for SMILES-only, ASO-only, or non-gene queries.

tools.name: Gene Set Enrichment (g:Profiler)
tools.purpose: Run functional enrichment analysis over a gene set using g:Profiler: top GO biological process, molecular function, and KEGG/Reactome pathway terms.
required_inputs: One or more gene symbols (a gene set or target + co-expressed genes)
tools.when_to_use: SELECT when the query involves multiple genes, pathway analysis, biological process enrichment, or when understanding the functional theme of a gene set adds value. Also useful for single-gene queries where pathway context matters. SKIP for SMILES-only or ASO-only queries.

tools.name: Robyn SCS Neuronal Connectivity
tools.purpose: Compute internal imaging-derived SCS/STA neuronal-connectivity scores from Quiver's robyn_scs model (requires v17_traces imaging plate data).
required_inputs: Imaging data path (v17_traces plate directory) — must be supplied explicitly in the run context
tools.when_to_use: SELECT only when imaging plate data (v17_traces) is explicitly present in the run context. SKIP for all standard text-query engagements with no imaging input — this tool has no useful output without the plate data.
